name: HS2 (High Speed 2)
description: UK flagship high-speed rail. Northern legs scrapped. Cost widely estimated to exceed £100bn. March 2026 parliamentary report: £43.6bn spent to Feb 2026 (£46.2bn inc. scrapped Phase 2). Earthworks 70% complete. Speed reduction under consideration (186mph vs 224mph). Revised cost estimate expected summer 2026.

name: Crossrail / Elizabeth Line
description: Elizabeth Line with severe delays and 4bn cost overrun. Opened 3.5 years late in May 2022.

name: Garden Bridge
description: Pedestrian bridge cancelled in 2017 after 53.5m of public money spent. Never built.

name: Hinkley Point C
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: Nuclear power station in Somerset. Budget escalated from £18bn to £48bn (nominal 2026 prices; £35bn in 2015 prices per EDF Feb 2026 update). Unit 1 first power now expected 2030, delayed from 2029 due to electromechanical work issues. EDF took €2.5bn impairment charge.

name: Ajax Armoured Vehicle
description: Troubled armoured vehicle programme with 10+ year delays. Excessive vibration causing soldier illness.

name: NHS National Programme for IT
description: One of the most expensive IT failures in government history. 12bn+ spent by dismantlement.

name: Universal Credit IT System
description: Welfare IT system. Cost increases from 2.2bn to 15.8bn. Multiple implementation delays.

name: Edinburgh Trams
description: Restricted tram line opened May 2014, nearly 500m over budget and 3 years late.

name: Scottish Parliament Building
description: Cost increased nearly 10x from 55m to 430m. Opened October 2004, 3 years late.

name: Millennium Dome
description: Total costs reached 789m at liquidation. Lottery funding 204m over estimate.

name: Wembley Stadium Rebuild
description: Opened March 2007, 3 years late. Budget overrun of 142%.

name: London 2012 Olympics
description: Cost 15bn, nearly 4x the 4bn bid budget. Delivered on time but massively over original estimates.

name: Thameslink Programme
description: London rail upgrade. Phase 2 increased 18% due to unexpected conditions.

name: A14 Cambridge to Huntingdon
description: A rare success story. Completed 8 months early and on budget at 1.5bn.

name: Smart Motorways Programme
description: Cancelled April 2023 citing safety concerns. 59.3m spent on scrapped schemes.

name: HMS Queen Elizabeth Carriers
description: UK two largest warships. Budget increased 85% from 4.1bn to 7.6bn.

name: Astute-Class Submarines
description: Nuclear attack submarines. Budget soared from 2.4bn to 12.2bn over 25+ years.

name: Type 26 Frigates
description: Anti-submarine warfare frigates. 8 ships planned. 233m cost growth with 12-month delay.

name: HS1 (Channel Tunnel Rail Link)
description: High-speed rail to St Pancras. Opened November 2007, 18% over budget.

name: Jubilee Line Extension
description: 67% budget overrun (1.4bn extra). 75 months vs 53 months planned.

name: Stonehenge Tunnel / A303
description: Cancelled March 2024. 179m already spent on planning before cancellation.

name: Thames Tideway Tunnel
description: 25km super sewer. Cost escalated from 1.7bn to 5bn over 20 years.

name: Northern Powerhouse Rail
description: Revived Jan 2026 with 1.1bn initial funding. 45bn Treasury cap may be insufficient.

name: E-Borders System
description: Border control IT cancelled after massive delays. 830m spent. Raytheon awarded 224m compensation.

name: Emergency Services Network
description: Troubled 11bn project to replace Airwave radio. First use now expected 2029+ vs 2017 target.

name: Lower Thames Crossing
description: 25km tunnel beneath Thames connecting Essex and Kent. Approved March 2025 after years of planning delays. Over 1.5bn spent before construction even began. Budget escalated from 4.4bn to 10.2bn. Opening pushed to 2034.

name: Northern Line Extension
description: Completed 160m under budget, a rare success. Opened September 2021.

name: Nimrod MRA4 Patrol Aircraft
description: Cancelled in 2010. 789m over budget and 9 years late. Only 3 aircraft built.

name: Post Office Horizon IT
description: Wrongly prosecuted 900+ subpostmasters (236 imprisoned). Over 1.48bn compensation paid.

name: Manchester Town Hall
description: Grade I listed building. 59% budget overrun from 330m to 525m.

name: Tempest / GCAP Fighter
description: Next-gen fighter jet with Italy and Japan. 2bn initial phase now 12bn+.

name: Heathrow Third Runway
description: Cost estimate risen from 14bn to 49bn. Massive opposition continues.

name: Sizewell C Nuclear Power Station
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: Twin EPR reactor nuclear station in Suffolk. FID taken July 2025. Government holds 44.9% equity. Cost estimates tripled from initial figures.

name: Great British Nuclear SMR Programme
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: Small Modular Reactor programme. Rolls-Royce SMR selected. First deployment planned at Wylfa, Wales. 2.5bn from GB Energy budget.

name: Carbon Capture & Storage Clusters
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: East Coast Cluster (Teesside) and HyNet (NW England/Wales). 21.7bn commitment over 25 years. Financial close Dec 2024.

name: GB Energy
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: New publicly-owned energy company. 8.3bn over parliament (5.8bn GB Energy, 2.5bn nuclear). Investment fund launching summer 2026.

name: National Grid Great Grid Upgrade
description: RIIO-T3 programme: 80+ major high-voltage cable projects. 35bn investment 2026-2031. Largest grid upgrade since 1950s.

name: Smart Meter Rollout
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: National smart meter installation. 70% coverage by Sept 2025. Deadline extended from 2020 to 2025 to 2030. Net benefit 3.7bn to date.

name: Green Homes Grant
department: Dept for Energy Security & Net Zero
description: Home insulation voucher scheme. Only 314m of 1.5bn voucher budget spent (21%). Closed after 6 months. Admin costs over 1k per home.

name: Dreadnought-Class Submarines
description: Four nuclear-armed submarines replacing Vanguard-class. 31bn budget + 10bn contingency. Unity reactor contract (9bn) awarded to Rolls-Royce.

name: Type 31 Inspiration-Class Frigates
description: Five general-purpose frigates. On budget at 1.25bn. Built at Rosyth. First steel cut 2021. Minor schedule slip.

name: Boxer Armoured Vehicles
description: 623 Boxer 8x8 vehicles for Mechanised Infantry. UK rejoined programme in 2018 after leaving in 2003. Assembly at Telford.

name: F-35B Lightning II
description: Stealth fighter procurement. MOD claims 18.76bn; NAO lifecycle estimate 71bn for 138 jets. Only 33 delivered so far. 52bn cost gap.

name: Challenger 3 Main Battle Tank
description: Upgrade of 148 Challenger 2 tanks with new turret and smoothbore gun. On budget and ahead of schedule.

name: AUKUS Submarine Programme
description: SSN-AUKUS nuclear-powered attack submarines with Australia. Analyst estimates exceed 40bn for UK share. Full cost not disclosed.

name: New Hospital Programme
department: Dept of Health and Social Care
description: 40 hospitals promise. Only 7 under construction by 2026. Cost escalated from 3.7bn to 15bn per wave. Programme split into waves.

name: NHS Federated Data Platform
description: Centralised NHS data analytics platform. 7-year contract. Controversial award after legal challenge. Budget risen to 485m.

name: Nightingale Hospitals
department: Dept of Health and Social Care
description: Seven emergency COVID hospitals. Cost 532m total. Only 3 of 7 were ever used for patients. All closed by 2021.

name: Affordable Homes Programme 2021-26
description: Flagship social housing programme. Budget topped up multiple times to 13.83bn. Target: 180,000 affordable homes by 2026.

name: Building Safety Fund (Post-Grenfell)
description: Removal of unsafe cladding from high-rise buildings. Budget grew from 1bn to 4.5bn. Actual remediation need estimated at 15bn+.

name: New Prisons Programme (10,000 places)
description: Pledge to build 10,000 new prison places. Budget risen from 2.5bn to 9.5bn. 5-year delay. Includes HMP Five Wells, Millsike, Welland Oaks.

name: School Rebuilding Programme
description: Target to rebuild 500 schools. Only 4% complete by 2026. Triggered by RAAC concrete crisis. Total need estimated at 20bn.

name: GOV.UK One Login
description: Single sign-on for all government services. 11m+ users, 80+ services integrated. On track. Replaced failed Verify programme.

name: Making Tax Digital
description: Digital tax reporting for businesses. Multiple delays. VAT phase live. Income Tax Self-Assessment launching April 2026.

name: Customs Declaration Service
description: Replacement for CHIEF customs system. Budget doubled from 157m to 325m. Delivered 4 years late. Now processing all UK declarations.

name: Verify Identity Programme
description: Government identity verification. Only 5.4m sign-ups vs 25m target. Budget overrun 42%. Replaced by GOV.UK One Login.

name: Police NLEDS (National Law Enforcement Data)
description: Replacement for Police National Computer. 68% budget overrun. 5+ years behind schedule. Critical national security system.

name: Great Western Electrification
description: Electrification of Great Western Main Line from Paddington to Cardiff. Cost ballooned from 1bn to 5.58bn. Swansea extension cancelled due to costs. NAO called it a case study in poor cost estimation.

name: Transpennine Route Upgrade
description: Railway upgrade connecting Manchester, Huddersfield, Leeds and York. Scope reduced multiple times yet costs still escalated from 2.9bn to 11.2bn. 3.9bn boost announced Dec 2023.

name: East West Rail
description: New rail link between Oxford and Cambridge. Western section Bicester-Bletchley opened 2024. Central section costs escalating. Original concept revived from closed Varsity Line.

name: Palace of Westminster Restoration
description: Comprehensive restoration of 19th-century Parliament building. Phase One alone costed at 3bn. Full programme estimates range 15.6bn to 39.2bn. Delay costs 70m per year. Fire, asbestos and structural risks mounting.

name: Type 45 Daring-Class Destroyers
description: Six guided-missile destroyers for Royal Navy. Order cut from 12 to 8 then to 6. 1.5bn overrun. Persistent propulsion failures required costly Power Improvement Programme retrofit.

name: Eurofighter Typhoon
description: Multi-role combat aircraft. UK received 160 aircraft. Massive cost overrun from 7bn to over 21bn for procurement alone. Per-unit cost tripled. Entered service 5 years late.

name: Midland Main Line Electrification
description: Electrification of Midland Main Line from London St Pancras to Kettering/Corby. Scope repeatedly cut back from full Sheffield route. Cost tripled from 500m to 1.5bn for reduced scope.

name: A66 Northern Trans-Pennine
description: Dualling of A66 between Penrith and Scotch Corner. Major trans-Pennine road upgrade. Budget nearly doubled from 1bn to 1.9bn. Construction underway on multiple sections.

name: Sellafield Decommissioning
description: Decommissioning of Europe's most complex nuclear site. Lifetime cost estimate tripled from 45bn to 136bn. Will take over a century to complete. Single largest UK government liability.

name: Project Gigabit (Broadband)
description: Gigabit-capable broadband to 85% of UK by 2025, 99% by 2030. Target downgraded from original universal full-fibre by 2025. Slow contract rollout in rural areas. 5bn public investment.

name: UK Digital Identity Programme
description: Biometric digital ID cards and verification system. 1.8bn budget announced in 2025 Budget. Controversial revival of national identity scheme scrapped in 2010 by Coalition government.

name: NHS Digital Transformation
description: Digitisation of NHS systems. NHS App as digital front door, single patient records, electronic prescriptions. Budget doubled to 10bn. Aims for 17bn in savings over 3 years through productivity gains.

name: Prison Estate Expansion (14,000 places)
description: 14,000 new prison places by 2031. Budget escalated from 5.9bn to 9.4bn driven by rapid deployment cells, 40% construction inflation and 1.8bn maintenance backlog across existing estate.

name: Connecting Europe Express / Channel Tunnel Upgrades
description: Channel Tunnel construction. Original 1985 estimate was 4.7bn, actual cost reached 9.5bn in 1994 prices. 80% overrun. Construction consortium nearly bankrupted. Largest privately-funded infrastructure project of its era.

name: Queensferry Crossing
contractors: Forth Crossing Bridge Constructors (Dragados, Hochtief, Samsung C&T)
description: Second Forth road crossing between Edinburgh and Fife. Cable-stayed bridge opened Aug 2017, £245m under budget. One of few UK megaprojects delivered below estimate.

name: Aberdeen Western Peripheral Route (AWPR)
description: Major bypass around Aberdeen. PPP contract originally £745m fixed-price, but delays, Carillion collapse, and utility conflicts pushed final cost past £1.2bn. 200%+ overrun on original estimate.

name: Bank Station Capacity Upgrade
description: Major expansion of London's busiest Underground station. New Northern line platform, entrance on Cannon Street, step-free access. 40% capacity increase. Completed Feb 2023 on budget.

name: A9 Dualling Programme
description: Upgrading 83 miles of A9 from Perth to Inverness to dual carriageway. Originally £3bn (2008 prices) for 2025 completion, now £3.97bn for 2035. MIM procurement abandoned as 28% more expensive than capital-funded approach.

name: Francis Crick Institute
description: Europe's largest biomedical research facility, Camden, London. 1,500 staff incl 1,250 scientists. UK Government contributed £220m, with MRC, Wellcome Trust, Cancer Research UK, Imperial and KCL as funders.

name: AWE Mensa Programme
contractors: AWE plc (now AWE Nuclear Security Technologies)
description: Warhead assembly/disassembly facility at AWE Burghfield. NAO found construction started before design was finalised, causing 148% cost overrun and 7-year delay. 48% of cost increase from immature designs.

name: A428 Black Cat to Caxton Gibbet
description: New 10-mile dual carriageway linking A1 (Bedfordshire) to A428 (Cambridgeshire). UK's largest road project currently under construction. Classified as Tier 1 nationally significant. Halfway point reached Oct 2025.

name: Silvertown Tunnel
contractors: Riverlinx CJV (BAM Nuttall, Ferrovial, SK Ecoplant)
description: New Thames crossing in east London connecting A102 to A406. 25-year DBFM PFI contract. TfL's direct capital exposure ~£179m; full 25-year availability payments estimated at ~£2bn. Opened April 2025.

name: Mersey Gateway Bridge
contractors: Merseylink consortium (Samsung C&T, FCC Construccion, Kier)
description: Cable-stayed bridge over River Mersey estuary. £600m capital construction cost; 30-year PPP total is £1.86bn (2014-2044) including operations and maintenance. Opened Oct 2017. Tolled crossing.

name: Luton DART
description: Automated people mover connecting Luton Airport Parkway station to terminal. Two years late and ~£75m over budget due to COVID, construction delays, and organisational problems. Opened March 2023.

name: Diamond Light Source
description: UK national synchrotron facility at Harwell, Oxfordshire. Phase I £263m (2007), Phase II £120m (2012), Phase III £111m (2021). 33+ beamlines operational. Joint-funded by STFC and Wellcome Trust.

name: HMNB Clyde Infrastructure Programme
description: Multi-billion modernisation of UK nuclear submarine base at Faslane/Coulport. Initial £250m enabling tranche (2025-2028) under "Clyde 2070" branding, preparing for Dreadnought-class. 10-year NEC4 framework valued £1.3bn; total programme estimated £1.9bn.

name: Warrior Capability Sustainment Programme (WCSP)
description: Upgrade of Warrior Infantry Fighting Vehicles cancelled March 2021 by Integrated Review. £594m spent before cancellation. Replacement by Boxer vehicles under separate programme.

name: Watchkeeper WK450 Drone
description: Tactical UAV programme for 54 aircraft. Over-specified with 1,910 individual criteria. Persistent software and airworthiness issues. 60% cost overrun before programme scrapped in 2023.

name: Voyager / Future Strategic Tanker Aircraft (FSTA)
description: 27-year PFI contract for 14 A330 MRTT tanker aircraft. Delivered on time. Total programme cost inc. MoD personnel and fuel estimated £12.3bn over contract life.

name: Apache AH-64E Attack Helicopter
description: Foreign Military Sale of 50 AH-64E Apache helicopters. Approved at £2bn in 2020, delivered at £1.79bn. One of few defence programmes delivered under budget.

name: Crowsnest AEW System
description: Searchwater radar on Merlin HM2 for Queen Elizabeth carriers. Budget ballooned from £269m to £425m+. Full operational capability still not achieved despite carrier strike deployments.

name: Skynet 5 Satellite Communications
description: PFI contract for military satellite communications constellation. Delivered under original budget. 4 satellites providing global comms. Contract extended to 2022.

name: Skynet 6 Satellite Communications
description: Next-generation military satellite communications through 2041. Skynet 6A satellite contract £500m+ signed 2020. Total programme value estimated £6bn inc. satellites, ground segment and services.

name: Protector RG Mk1 Drone
description: MQ-9B remotely piloted aircraft. Whole-life cost up from £1.24bn Main Gate to £1.76bn by Mar 2023. £520m overrun (42%). First aircraft arrived UK 2024 amid continued delays.

name: E-7 Wedgetail Airborne Early Warning
description: 5 Boeing E-7 aircraft replacing E-3D Sentry. Core contract US.98bn (£1.51bn) plus £300m for Northrop Grumman radar and £83m RAF Lossiemouth works. IOC expected 2026.

name: Morpheus Tactical Communications
description: Next-gen tactical comms for Land forces. £3.2bn through-life budget. £828m spent by 2024. General Dynamics contract terminated Dec 2023 for non-performance. Programme being restructured.

name: Borders Railway
description: 30-mile railway with 7 new stations, Edinburgh to Tweedbank. Delivered on time Sept 2015. 20% above original estimate but within revised budget. 13m journeys in first decade.

name: M4 Relief Road (Newport)
description: Proposed relief road around Newport, Wales. Budget escalated to £1.4bn. Cancelled June 2019 by First Minister. Over £150m spent on preparatory work and land acquisition before abandonment.

name: Crossrail 2
description: Proposed north-south London rail line. Cost est £27-32bn (2014 prices). Shelved Nov 2020 as condition of emergency COVID TfL funding deal. £115m+ spent on development before shelving.

name: Nottingham Express Transit Phase 2
description: Two tram extensions (17km) to Clifton and Toton. Total cost £570m, DfT funding £371m. Completed Aug 2015, 8 months late but on budget. Funded partly from Workplace Parking Levy.

name: Defence Information Infrastructure (DII)
description: Integrated defence IT network. Parliament told £2.3bn; actual cost £7.09bn by 2015 (208% overrun). NAO found immature designs, weak oversight. Delivered only 29,000 of 63,000 terminals by 2008.

name: HMCTS Reform Programme
description: Courts and tribunals digital reform. Original £1bn, extended to £1.3bn. Common Platform case management system troubled. HMCTS expects £2bn lifetime savings. NAO flagged delivery risks.

name: Geological Disposal Facility (GDF)
department: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
description: Deep geological repository for UK higher-activity radioactive waste. Mid-range est £20bn, high-end £54bn. HM Treasury rated "unachievable" in 2025 due to major issues with scope, schedule and cost.

name: Magnox Decommissioning Programme
description: Decommissioning of Magnox nuclear fleet across UK. Original 2014 bid £3.8bn, rose to £6bn by 2017, latest est £8.7bn. Multi-decade programme. NAO found original competition wasted £122m.

name: Royal Liverpool University Hospital
department: Department of Health and Social Care
description: PFI hospital collapsed with Carillion Jan 2018. Construction cost doubled from £350m to £724m. Govt paid £42m compensation to terminate PFI. Total taxpayer cost £739m. Opened 6+ years late.

name: Midland Metropolitan University Hospital
department: Department of Health and Social Care
description: PFI hospital hit by Carillion collapse after £205m spent. Construction cost doubled to £709m taxpayer share. Handed to Skanska. Originally due Oct 2018, opened Oct 2024. 6-year delay.

name: Wylfa Newydd Nuclear Power Station
department: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
description: Proposed 2,700 MWe ABWR station on Anglesey. Hitachi suspended Jan 2019. Horizon withdrew planning application Jan 2021. Government had committed £5bn direct funding. ~£2bn spent before suspension.

name: Moorside Nuclear Power Station
department: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
description: Proposed 3.4 GW nuclear station in Cumbria. Toshiba cancelled via NuGen subsidiary Nov 2018, absorbing £100.5m loss. Would have required £2.8bn National Grid infrastructure.

name: NHS Test and Trace
department: Dept of Health and Social Care
description: COVID-19 test and trace programme. NAO found over 900 consultants charged up to £1,000/day. Programme cost £37bn with widely questioned value for money. £1.2bn on unused antivirals.
cronyNote: Test & Trace contracts awarded to Deloitte, Serco, BCG via emergency procurement

name: COVID PPE Procurement
department: Dept of Health and Social Care
description: Emergency PPE procurement during COVID. NAO found £4bn of unusable PPE to be destroyed, £762m unlikely to be recovered, 52% of surgical gowns non-compliant. 135 contracts flagged with 3+ corruption red flags.
cronyNote: PPE contracts awarded through VIP Lane to politically connected firms

name: Bibby Stockholm Asylum Barge
description: Floating asylum accommodation in Portland, Dorset. Monthly cost £6,000/person due to under-occupancy. Contract not renewed after January 2025. Setup and operational costs exceeded hotel accommodation rates.
cronyNote: Asylum accommodation contract scrutinised for value for money

name: RAF Scampton Asylum Site
description: Planned asylum processing centre at former RAF Scampton in Lincolnshire. £60m spent on development before plans were scrapped in September 2024. PAC found nearly £100m wasted across failed asylum housing projects.
cronyNote: Asylum site development costs questioned by PAC

name: Carer's Allowance Overpayments
description: DWP systemic failure to flag overpayments to carers. £357m in wrongful overpayments between 2018-2024. Cliff-edge rules meant earning 1p over threshold lost entire week's allowance. ~600 carers faced criminal prosecution.

name: A27 Arundel Bypass
description: Proposed bypass around Arundel in West Sussex. Cancelled July 2024 as part of government road programme review. Development costs written off.

name: A358 Taunton to Southfields
description: Proposed dual carriageway upgrade between Taunton and Southfields. Cancelled October 2024 in roads review. Development costs written off as part of £2.7bn DfT write-off programme.

name: A1 Morpeth to Ellingham Dualling
description: Proposed dualling of the A1 between Morpeth and Ellingham in Northumberland. Cancelled October 2024 in roads review. Part of broader DfT project cancellation programme.

name: HS2 Phase 2 (Northern Legs)
description: Phase 2a (West Midlands to Crewe) and Phase 2b (Crewe to Manchester, Birmingham to Leeds) cancelled by PM in October 2023. Billions spent on planning, land acquisition, and early works written off. Network Rail Overhaul announced as partial replacement.

name: Rwanda Asylum Partnership
description: Agreement to send asylum seekers to Rwanda for processing. £700m+ spent including £290m paid directly to Rwanda, legal costs, and operational setup. No deportation flights ever took place. Scheme scrapped by incoming government July 2024.

name: COVID Bounce Back Loan Scheme Fraud
description: Government-backed emergency loans to small businesses during COVID. NAO estimated £4.9bn in fraud losses and £1.1bn in credit losses due to minimal checks at application. Loan scheme design prioritised speed over fraud prevention.
